tagline: Track recommended weight gain during pregnancy
keywords: pregnancy weight gain, prenatal weight, BMI pregnancy
calcFn: var ranges = preBmi < 18.5 ? [28,40] : preBmi < 25 ? [25,35] : preBmi < 30 ? [15,25] : [11,20]; var weeklyRate = preBmi < 25 ? 1 : 0.6; var firstTri = weeks <= 13 ? weeks * 0.3 : 4; var laterWeeks = Math.max(weeks - 13, 0); var recommended = firstTri + laterWeeks * weeklyRate; var totalRange = ranges[0] + '-' + ranges[1]; return {recommended: fmt(recommended,1), totalRange: totalRange, weeklyRate: fmt(weeklyRate,1)};

faq.q: How much weight should I gain?
faq.a: 25-35 lbs for normal BMI, 15-25 for overweight, 28-40 for underweight.

faq.q: When does most weight gain occur?
faq.a: Most weight gain happens in the 2nd and 3rd trimesters.

formulas.name: Weekly Rate
formulas.explanation: Steady gain after the first trimester.

tagline: Track and time fetal movement counts
keywords: kick count, fetal movement, baby kicks, pregnancy monitoring
calcFn: var rate = (kicks / minutes) * 60; var status = kicks >= 10 && minutes <= 120 ? 'Normal' : kicks >= 10 ? 'Slow but reached 10' : 'Below threshold'; var action = kicks < 10 && minutes >= 120 ? 'Contact your healthcare provider' : kicks < 10 ? 'Keep counting - try for 10 in 2 hours' : 'Normal - no action needed'; return {rate: fmt(rate,1), status: status, action: action};

faq.q: How many kicks should I feel?
faq.a: At least 10 movements in 2 hours is the standard guideline.

faq.q: When should I start counting?
faq.a: Most providers recommend starting kick counts at 28 weeks.

formulas.name: Kick Rate
formulas.explanation: Kicks per hour from the counting session.

tagline: Know how long stored breastmilk is safe to use
keywords: breastmilk storage, pumped milk, breast milk, storage time
calcFn: var maxTimes = {'Room Temperature': 4, 'Insulated Cooler': 24, 'Refrigerator': 96, 'Freezer (attached)': 4320, 'Deep Freezer': 8760}; var maxHours = maxTimes[storage] || 4; var remaining = maxHours - hoursStored; var status = remaining > maxHours * 0.25 ? 'Safe to use' : remaining > 0 ? 'Use soon' : 'Discard - past safe window'; return {maxHours: maxHours <= 96 ? fmt(maxHours,0) : fmt(maxHours/24,0) + ' (' + fmt(maxHours/24,0) + ' days)', remaining: remaining > 0 ? (remaining <= 96 ? fmt(remaining,0) : fmt(remaining/24,0) + ' days') : '0', status: status};

faq.q: How long can breastmilk sit out?
faq.a: Up to 4 hours at room temperature, 24 hours in a cooler, 4 days refrigerated.

faq.q: Can I refreeze thawed breastmilk?
faq.a: No, once thawed it should be used within 24 hours and never refrozen.

formulas.name: Time Remaining
formulas.formula: Remaining = Max Storage Time - Hours Stored
formulas.explanation: Simple countdown from maximum safe duration.

keywords: baby formula, infant formula, formula cost, feeding cost
calcFn: var ozPerFeeding = ageMonths < 1 ? 2.5 : ageMonths < 3 ? 4 : ageMonths < 6 ? 6 : 8; var ozPerDay = ozPerFeeding * feedingsPerDay; var costs = {'Standard Powder ($0.15/oz)': 0.15, 'Organic ($0.22/oz)': 0.22, 'Specialty/Hypoallergenic ($0.30/oz)': 0.30, 'Ready-to-Feed ($0.35/oz)': 0.35}; var costPerOz = costs[formulaType] || 0.15; var monthlyCost = ozPerDay * costPerOz * 30; var annualCost = monthlyCost * 12; return {ozPerDay: fmt(ozPerDay,0), monthlyCost: dollar(monthlyCost), annualCost: dollar(annualCost)};

faq.q: How much formula does a baby need?
faq.a: Roughly 2.5 oz per pound of body weight per day, usually 24-32 oz total.

faq.q: Which formula is cheapest?
faq.a: Store-brand powder is most economical, meeting the same FDA nutritional standards.

formulas.name: Daily Formula
formulas.formula: Daily oz = oz per feeding × feedings per day
formulas.explanation: Total daily consumption from feeding schedule.

tagline: Check if your nursery paint VOC levels are safe for baby
keywords: nursery paint, VOC, baby safe, low VOC, nursery safety
calcFn: var rating = vocLevel <= 5 ? 'Excellent (zero/ultra-low VOC)' : vocLevel <= 50 ? 'Good (low VOC)' : vocLevel <= 150 ? 'Fair (medium VOC)' : 'Poor (high VOC)'; var baseWait = vocLevel <= 5 ? 1 : vocLevel <= 50 ? 3 : vocLevel <= 150 ? 7 : 14; var ventMult = {'Poor (no windows open)': 2, 'Moderate (1 window)': 1.5, 'Good (cross ventilation)': 1, 'Excellent (fans + windows)': 0.7}; var waitTime = Math.ceil(baseWait * (ventMult[ventilation] || 1)); var recommendation = vocLevel <= 50 ? 'Safe choice for nursery' : 'Consider a lower VOC option for baby safety'; return {rating: rating, waitTime: fmt(waitTime,0), recommendation: recommendation};

faq.q: What VOC level is safe for a nursery?
faq.a: Choose zero-VOC (< 5 g/L) or low-VOC (< 50 g/L) paints for nurseries.

faq.q: How long should paint cure before baby arrives?
faq.a: At minimum 3-7 days with good ventilation. Zero-VOC paint needs only 1-2 days.

formulas.name: Wait Time
formulas.explanation: VOC-based wait time adjusted for room ventilation.

keywords: postpartum recovery, after birth, healing, postpartum timeline
calcFn: var baseWeeks = {'Vaginal (uncomplicated)': 4, 'Vaginal (with tearing)': 6, 'C-Section': 8}; var physicalRecovery = baseWeeks[delivery] || 6; var exerciseReturn = physicalRecovery + 2; var supportMod = {'Strong support': 0, 'Some help': 1, 'Minimal support': 3}; var fullRecovery = physicalRecovery + 4 + (supportMod[support] || 0); return {physicalRecovery: fmt(physicalRecovery,0), exerciseReturn: fmt(exerciseReturn,0), fullRecovery: fmt(fullRecovery,0)};

faq.q: How long is postpartum recovery?
faq.a: 4-6 weeks for vaginal delivery, 6-8 weeks for C-section, with full recovery taking longer.

faq.q: When can I exercise after birth?
faq.a: Light walking immediately, but wait 6-8 weeks (vaginal) or 8-10 weeks (C-section) for more.

tagline: Track and score your daily mood patterns
keywords: mood tracker, mood score, mental health, daily mood
calcFn: var anxietyInv = 11 - anxiety; var score = ((overallMood + anxietyInv + energy + sleepQuality + socialConnection) / 50) * 100; var assessment = score >= 80 ? 'Thriving - keep doing what works' : score >= 60 ? 'Good - stable mood' : score >= 40 ? 'Fair - consider self-care focus' : 'Low - reach out for support'; return {score: fmt(score,0), assessment: assessment};

faq.q: Why track mood daily?
faq.a: Tracking reveals patterns, triggers, and trends that help you manage mental health proactively.

faq.q: What affects mood most?
faq.a: Sleep, exercise, social connection, and stress are the biggest controllable mood factors.

formulas.name: Wellness Score
formulas.explanation: Five-factor composite with anxiety inverted (lower is better).

tagline: Estimate recovery time from stressful events
keywords: stress recovery, burnout, stress management, cortisol
calcFn: var baseDays = {'Acute (hours)': 1, 'Short-term (days)': 7, 'Chronic (weeks/months)': 30}; var base = baseDays[duration] || 7; var severityMult = stressLevel / 5; var copingMult = {'Many (exercise, therapy, support)': 0.6, 'Some (1-2 methods)': 1, 'Few (limited resources)': 1.5}; var recoveryDays = Math.ceil(base * severityMult * (copingMult[copingTools] || 1)); var priority = stressLevel >= 7 ? 'Seek professional support, prioritize sleep and movement' : 'Focus on rest, social connection, and routine'; return {recoveryDays: fmt(recoveryDays,0), priority: priority};

faq.q: How long does stress recovery take?
faq.a: Acute stress: hours to days. Chronic stress: weeks to months with intentional recovery.

faq.q: What helps recovery most?
faq.a: Sleep, exercise, social support, therapy, and reducing stressors.

formulas.name: Recovery Time
formulas.explanation: Duration-based estimate adjusted for severity and available coping tools.

tagline: Assess the mental health impact of your screen time
keywords: screen time, digital wellness, phone usage, mental health
calcFn: var riskScore = 0; riskScore += totalHours > 8 ? 3 : totalHours > 5 ? 2 : totalHours > 3 ? 1 : 0; riskScore += socialMedia > 3 ? 3 : socialMedia > 2 ? 2 : socialMedia > 1 ? 1 : 0; riskScore += beforeBed > 60 ? 2 : beforeBed > 30 ? 1 : 0; var riskLevel = riskScore >= 6 ? 'High' : riskScore >= 3 ? 'Moderate' : 'Low'; var sleepImpact = beforeBed > 60 ? 'Significant - blue light delays melatonin by 30+ min' : beforeBed > 30 ? 'Moderate - consider blue light filter' : 'Minimal'; var recommendation = riskScore >= 6 ? 'Reduce social media by 1 hr, no screens 1 hr before bed' : riskScore >= 3 ? 'Set screen time limits, use night mode after sunset' : 'Healthy screen habits - maintain current patterns'; return {riskLevel: riskLevel, sleepImpact: sleepImpact, recommendation: recommendation};

faq.q: How much screen time is too much?
faq.a: Research suggests >2 hours of social media daily is associated with increased anxiety and depression.

faq.q: Does screen time before bed matter?
faq.a: Yes, blue light suppresses melatonin production, delaying sleep onset by 20-60 minutes.

formulas.name: Risk Score
formulas.formula: Score = Total + Social Media + Before-Bed factors
formulas.explanation: Composite risk from three screen time dimensions.

tagline: Assess your social health and connection quality
keywords: social connection, loneliness, social health, relationships
calcFn: var relScore = Math.min(closeRelationships * 5, 25); var interScore = Math.min(weeklyInteractions * 3, 25); var belongScore = belongingScore * 2.5; var loneScores = {'Rarely': 25, 'Sometimes': 15, 'Often': 5, 'Always': 0}; var loneScore = loneScores[loneliness] || 10; var score = relScore + interScore + belongScore + loneScore; var assessment = score >= 80 ? 'Strong social health' : score >= 60 ? 'Adequate - room for deeper connections' : score >= 40 ? 'At risk - prioritize social activities' : 'Concerning - consider reaching out for support'; return {score: fmt(score,0), assessment: assessment};

faq.q: Why does social connection matter?
faq.a: Strong social ties reduce mortality risk by 50%, comparable to quitting smoking.

faq.q: How many close relationships do I need?
faq.a: Research suggests 3-5 close relationships is optimal for mental health.

formulas.name: Social Score
formulas.formula: Score = Relationships + Interactions + Belonging + Loneliness factors

tagline: Assess your risk of professional burnout
keywords: burnout, burnout risk, work stress, exhaustion, work-life balance
calcFn: var workScore = workHours > 55 ? 25 : workHours > 45 ? 15 : workHours > 40 ? 8 : 0; var exhaustScore = exhaustion * 2.5; var detachScore = detachment * 2; var accomScore = (10 - accomplishment) * 1.5; var vacScore = vacation < 2 ? 10 : vacation < 5 ? 5 : 0; var riskScore = Math.min(workScore + exhaustScore + detachScore + accomScore + vacScore, 100); var level = riskScore >= 70 ? 'High' : riskScore >= 45 ? 'Moderate' : riskScore >= 25 ? 'Low' : 'Minimal'; var action = riskScore >= 70 ? 'Urgent: take time off, set boundaries, consider therapy' : riskScore >= 45 ? 'Set work boundaries, schedule regular breaks' : 'Maintain current balance, monitor stress levels'; return {riskScore: fmt(riskScore,0), level: level, action: action};

faq.q: What are the signs of burnout?
faq.a: Chronic exhaustion, cynicism, detachment, reduced performance, and physical symptoms.

faq.q: How to prevent burnout?
faq.a: Set boundaries, take regular time off, exercise, maintain social connections outside work.

formulas.name: Burnout Score
formulas.formula: Score = Work Hours + Exhaustion + Detachment + Low Accomplishment + No Vacation

tagline: Estimate mental health benefits of a gratitude practice
keywords: gratitude, gratitude journal, positive psychology, mental health
calcFn: var freqMult = {'Daily': 7, '3-4 times per week': 3.5, 'Weekly': 1, 'Rarely': 0.25}; var sessionsPerWeek = freqMult[frequency] || 1; var totalEntries = Math.round(sessionsPerWeek * duration * items); var consistency = Math.min(sessionsPerWeek / 7 * 100, 100); var wellnessBoost = Math.min(Math.sqrt(duration) * sessionsPerWeek * 1.2, 25); return {consistency: fmt(consistency,0), wellnessBoost: fmt(wellnessBoost,0), totalEntries: fmt(totalEntries,0)};

faq.q: Does gratitude practice actually work?
faq.a: Research shows regular gratitude practice improves wellbeing by 10-25% over 8+ weeks.

faq.q: How long until I see benefits?
faq.a: Most studies show measurable improvements within 3-4 weeks of daily practice.

formulas.name: Entries
formulas.explanation: Total gratitude items recorded over the practice period.

tagline: Calculate the cost to charge your electric vehicle at home
keywords: EV charging, electric car, charging cost, kWh, Level 2
calcFn: var kwhNeeded = batteryKwh * (targetCharge - currentCharge) / 100 / 0.9; var chargeCost = kwhNeeded * electricRate; var totalRange = batteryKwh * (targetCharge/100) * 3.5; var costPerMile = (chargeCost / (kwhNeeded * 0.9 * 3.5)) * 100; return {kwhNeeded: fmt(kwhNeeded,1), chargeCost: dollar(chargeCost), costPerMile: fmt(costPerMile,1)};

faq.q: How much does it cost to charge an EV?
faq.a: Typically $5-15 for a full charge at home, depending on battery size and electricity rate.

faq.q: Level 1 vs Level 2 charging?
faq.a: Level 1 (120V) adds 3-5 miles/hour. Level 2 (240V) adds 25-30 miles/hour.

formulas.name: Charge Cost
formulas.explanation: Energy needed accounting for 90% charging efficiency times electric rate.

tagline: Compare heat pump costs to traditional furnace heating
keywords: heat pump, furnace, heating cost, HVAC, energy savings
calcFn: var dailyBtu = sqft * 25; var annualBtu = dailyBtu * heatingDays; var therms = annualBtu / 100000 / 0.95; var furnaceCost = therms * gasRate; var kwhNeeded = annualBtu / 3412 / 3; var heatPumpCost = kwhNeeded * electricRate; var savings = furnaceCost - heatPumpCost; return {furnaceCost: dollar(furnaceCost), heatPumpCost: dollar(heatPumpCost), savings: dollar(savings)};

faq.q: Are heat pumps cheaper to run?
faq.a: Usually yes, heat pumps are 2-3x more efficient. Savings depend on gas vs electric prices.

faq.q: Do heat pumps work in cold climates?
faq.a: Modern cold-climate heat pumps work efficiently down to -15°F.

formulas.name: COP Advantage
formulas.explanation: Heat pumps deliver 3x the energy they consume.

tagline: Calculate how long until your solar panels pay for themselves
keywords: solar payback, solar ROI, solar panel cost, solar savings
calcFn: var netCost = systemCost * (1 - taxCredit/100); var annualSavings = monthlyBill * 12 * (offsetPct/100); var paybackYears = netCost / annualSavings; return {netCost: dollar(netCost), annualSavings: dollar(annualSavings), paybackYears: fmt(paybackYears,1)};

faq.q: How long do solar panels take to pay off?
faq.a: Typically 6-12 years with the federal tax credit, depending on electricity costs.

faq.q: What is the federal solar tax credit?
faq.a: 30% of system cost through 2032, then stepping down.

formulas.name: Payback
formulas.formula: Years = (System Cost × (1 - Tax Credit)) / Annual Savings
formulas.explanation: Net cost divided by yearly electricity savings.

tagline: Estimate savings from common home energy improvements
keywords: energy audit, home energy, efficiency, energy savings
calcFn: var savingsPct = 0; savingsPct += insulation === 'Yes' ? 15 : 0; savingsPct += airSeal === 'Yes' ? 10 : 0; savingsPct += ledLights === 'Yes' ? 5 : 0; savingsPct += smartThermo === 'Yes' ? 8 : 0; var monthlySavings = monthlyBill * (savingsPct/100); var annualSavings = monthlySavings * 12; return {savingsPct: fmt(savingsPct,0), monthlySavings: dollar(monthlySavings), annualSavings: dollar(annualSavings)};

faq.q: What saves the most energy?
faq.a: Insulation and air sealing typically save 15-25% on heating/cooling costs.

faq.q: Are energy audits worth it?
faq.a: Professional audits cost $200-400 but typically identify $200-1000+/year in savings.

formulas.name: Savings
formulas.explanation: Each improvement contributes a percentage reduction to energy bills.

tagline: Calculate the R-value insulation you need for your climate zone
keywords: R-value, insulation, energy efficiency, climate zone, attic insulation
calcFn: var rValues = {'Attic Floor': {'Zone 1 (Hot - Miami)': 30, 'Zone 2 (Hot - Houston)': 38, 'Zone 3 (Mixed-Hot - Atlanta)': 38, 'Zone 4 (Mixed - DC)': 49, 'Zone 5 (Cold - Chicago)': 49, 'Zone 6 (Cold - Minneapolis)': 60, 'Zone 7 (Very Cold - Duluth)': 60}, 'Exterior Wall': {'Zone 1 (Hot - Miami)': 13, 'Zone 2 (Hot - Houston)': 13, 'Zone 3 (Mixed-Hot - Atlanta)': 20, 'Zone 4 (Mixed - DC)': 20, 'Zone 5 (Cold - Chicago)': 20, 'Zone 6 (Cold - Minneapolis)': 21, 'Zone 7 (Very Cold - Duluth)': 21}, 'Crawl Space': {'Zone 1 (Hot - Miami)': 13, 'Zone 2 (Hot - Houston)': 19, 'Zone 3 (Mixed-Hot - Atlanta)': 19, 'Zone 4 (Mixed - DC)': 25, 'Zone 5 (Cold - Chicago)': 25, 'Zone 6 (Cold - Minneapolis)': 30, 'Zone 7 (Very Cold - Duluth)': 30}, 'Basement Wall': {'Zone 1 (Hot - Miami)': 0, 'Zone 2 (Hot - Houston)': 0, 'Zone 3 (Mixed-Hot - Atlanta)': 5, 'Zone 4 (Mixed - DC)': 10, 'Zone 5 (Cold - Chicago)': 15, 'Zone 6 (Cold - Minneapolis)': 15, 'Zone 7 (Very Cold - Duluth)': 15}}; var recommended = rValues[area][zone] || 30; var additional = Math.max(recommended - existing, 0); var fiberglass = additional / 3.2; return {recommended: 'R-' + fmt(recommended,0), additional: 'R-' + fmt(additional,0), fiberglass: fmt(fiberglass,1)};

faq.q: What R-value do I need?
faq.a: Depends on climate zone and location in home. Attics need R-30 to R-60.

faq.q: What is R-value?
faq.a: A measure of thermal resistance. Higher R-value = better insulation.

formulas.name: Additional R-Value
formulas.explanation: The gap between what you have and what is recommended.

tagline: Calculate how long your generator will run on a tank of fuel
keywords: generator runtime, generator fuel, portable generator, backup power
calcFn: var fullLoadGPH = ratedWatts / 1000 * 0.75; var actualGPH = fullLoadGPH * (loadPct/100); var runtime = tankSize / actualGPH; var costPerHour = actualGPH * 3.80; return {runtime: fmt(runtime,1), fuelPerHour: fmt(actualGPH,2), costPerHour: dollar(costPerHour)};

faq.q: How long will a generator run?
faq.a: A typical 3500W generator runs 8-12 hours on 5 gallons at 50% load.

faq.q: Should I run at full load?
faq.a: No, running at 50-75% load is more fuel efficient and extends generator life.

formulas.name: Runtime
formulas.explanation: Tank capacity divided by fuel consumption rate at current load.
